title: Ishvara Temple, Arasikere
tagline: Ishvara Temple, Arasikere — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Ishvara temple, also referred to as the Ishwara or Isvara temple, is an early 13th-century Hindu temple in Arsikere, Hassan district, Karnataka India.
overview.description: The Ishvara temple, also referred to as the Ishwara or Isvara temple, is an early 13th-century Hindu temple in Arsikere, Hassan district, Karnataka India. Dedicated to Shiva, it is one of the most notable early Hoysala architecture examples with a rotating circular plan, a domed mandapa with a 16-point star shape, a pancatala vimana, and a galaxy of artwork depicting Shaivism, Vaishnavism, Shaktism and Vedic legends of Hinduism. The Ishvara temple was one among a complex of many Hindu temples in Arasikere. Most of these and their artwork were destroyed and mutilated in or after the 14th-centur

topPlaces.name: Arsikere
topPlaces.description: Arasikere is a city and taluka in the Hassan district in the state of Karnataka, India. Arsikere also called as Siddara Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Aggunda
topPlaces.description: Aggunda is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Arsikere taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Bendekere
topPlaces.description: Bendekere is a village in Hassan district in Karnataka state, India. It is situated 5km away from sub-district headquarter Arsikere and 50km away from district headquarter Hassan.

topPlaces.name: Adihalli (Channarayapatna)
topPlaces.description: Adihalli (Channarayapatna) is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Channarayapatna taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Ajjanahalli, Arsikere
topPlaces.description: Ajjanahalli, Arsikere is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Arsikere taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Someshvara Temple, Haranhalli
topPlaces.description: The Someshvara temple at Haranhalli, sometimes referred to as Somesvara temple of Haranhalli, is one of two major historic Hindu temples that have survived in Haranhalli, Karnataka, India. It is dedicated to Shiva, while the other – Lakshminarasimha Temple, Haranhalli few hundred meters to the west – is dedicated to Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Lakshminarasimha Temple, Haranhalli
topPlaces.description: The Lakshminarasimha temple at Haranhalli, sometimes referred to as Lakshmi Narasimha temple of Haranhalli, is one of two major historic Hindu temples that have survived in Haranhalli, Karnataka, India. It is triple-shrine temple dedicated to Vishnu, while the other – Someshvara Temple, Haranhalli few hundred meters to the east – is dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Adihalli (Arsikere)
topPlaces.description: Adihalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Arsikere taluk of Hassan district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ishvara Temple, Arasikere?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ishvara Temple, Arasikere is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ishvara Temple, Arasikere?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (128 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (119 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ishvara Temple, Arasikere?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ishvara Temple, Arasikere at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ishvara Temple, Arasikere?
faq.a: Stays in Ishvara Temple, Arasikere start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ishvara Temple, Arasikere?
faq.a: The top attractions are Arsikere, Aggunda, Bendekere, Adihalli (Channarayapatna), Ajjanahalli, Arsikere.
